title: Protected categories and the problem of self-identification in transgender law
description: Debates around proposed amendments to the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act have largely been framed as a contest between two positions: the autonomy of self-identification on the one hand and the risk of State control through certification and verification on the other. This framing, while important, remains incomplete. It overlooks a prior question that law must necessarily confront: how does a legal system identify and structure protection in the absence of stable categories?The
